stepAnswer: No. A high value of $$r$$ does not necessarily imply that the relationship between the variables is linear.
stepTitle: (d) Suppose you never generated the scatter plot at the beginning of this section. Knowing only that the value of $$r$$ is roughly $$0.73$$, can you assume that the two variables have a linear association?
answerLatex: No. A high value of $$r$$ does not necessarily imply that the relationship between the variables is linear.
choices: No, the value of $$r$$ $$=$$ $$0.73$$ is not high enough to imply a linear association.
